seo.title: Pika 2.2 Text-to-Video: 5s/10s, 1080p & Pricing
seo.description: Pika 2.2 Text-to-Video on MaxVideoAI: silent 5s or 10s stylized clips in 720p/1080p. See pricing, limits, prompts and examples you can recreate.

overview: Pika 2.2 in MaxVideoAI: silent, stylized Text-to-Video clips at 720p/1080p for 5s or 10s social loops. Ideal for anime, comic, pixel and playful 3D looks built for feeds.

hero.title: Pika 2.2
hero.intro: Max video length: 5s or 10s in 720p/1080p — stylized Text-to-Video built to stand out in the feed.

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text → Video first; use the related Pika image workflow only when one still must anchor composition

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: No audio track is generated (silent by design)

technicalOverview.label: Controls
technicalOverview.body: Seeds and negative prompts to keep style consistent and suppress artifacts

promptStructure.title: Text-to-Video with Pika 2.2
promptStructure.quote: Stylized [style] animation of [subject] in [environment]. Camera [movement] while [key action] happens over [5/10] seconds, [aspect ratio], with [effects]. No audio.
promptStructure.description: Write prompts like a stylized animation brief: subject + style, environment, motion, effects, framing and duration.
steps: Subject & style (anime, pixel-art, comic, cel-shaded 3D, etc.)
Framing & duration (close/medium/wide; 5 or 10 seconds; aspect ratio)

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Lean into stylized looks (anime, comic, pixel, toon, stylized 3D)
Keep clips short and high-energy for feeds and overlays
Use seeds and negative prompts to refine motion and clean artifacts
Silent output: add your own music/VO in post or rerun in an audio-capable engine
Keep one subject, one environment and one main effect per clip for clarity

prompts.title: Anime courier rooftop run (10s, 9:16)
prompts.prompt: Anime-style vertical shot of a teen courier sprinting across neon rooftops at dusk, 10s, 9:16. Camera tracks beside them as holographic billboards whoosh past, smear-frame trails on fast limbs, soft parallax clouds, no logos, no on-screen text.

prompts.title: Pixel wizard loop (5s, 1:1)
prompts.prompt: 5s seamless pixel-art loop of a tiny wizard stirring a glowing cauldron in a cozy 1:1 cottage interior. Camera locked medium shot. Bubbles rise in a repeating pattern, books float gently, warm candlelight, chunky retro pixels, no text or logos.

faqs.a: No. Pika 2.2 in MaxVideoAI always outputs silent clips. Add sound in post or use an audio-capable engine when needed.

faqs.q: Which duration should I use: 5 or 10 seconds?
faqs.a: 5s for punchy hooks and quick loops; 10s when you need a bit more room for build-up or parallax.
Yes. Choose 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, Shorts and Stories; keep the subject centered for safe crops.

faqs.q: How do seeds and negative prompts help?
faqs.a: Reuse a seed to keep iterations related; use negative prompts to block text/logos/extra characters/watermarks.

faqs.q: When should I use another engine instead?
faqs.a: For photoreal people/products or built-in sound, use Sora 2 or Veo 3.1; for sound-ready short clips, consider Wan 2.5.

custom.heroDesc1: Use Pika 2.2 to generate stylized 5s or 10s Text-to-Video clips in up to 1080p. See the price per clip before you run, then recreate proven examples in one click.
custom.heroDesc2: Pick 5 or 10 seconds, choose 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:5, 5:4, 3:2 or 2:3, write a compact prompt, and get a silent clip ready for your edit.
custom.heroSupportLine: Best when you want a strong look fast: moodboards, loops, cutaways, and playful creative variants.
heroHighlights: Stylized motion that reads instantly||Great for bold looks (anime/comic/toon/experimental) without chasing photorealism.
Consistency controls||Seed + negative prompt let you keep a look stable and suppress unwanted artifacts across variants.
Prompt-led variants||Seed and negative prompt controls help you test style loops without overloading the setup.
Silent by design (edit-friendly)||Lightweight exports you can score however you like in post.
custom.whatTitle: What Pika 2.2 Actually Is in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Pika 2.2 is a short-form, stylized video model tuned for social-first animation.
custom.whatIntro2: In MaxVideoAI it’s wired as a silent engine with tight control over duration, resolution and aspect ratio.
whatFlowSteps: 2. Choose Text → Video for this page; use the related image-start workflow only when a still must anchor composition.
3. Set duration (5/10 s), aspect ratio and resolution (720p or 1080p).
4. (Optional) Set a seed and add a negative prompt.
6. See the price per clip before you run the job.
custom.specNote: These specs describe Pika 2.2 exactly as you can use it today via MaxVideoAI.

specSections.title: Stylized looks
specSections.intro: Optimized for bold, social-first aesthetics over photorealism. It excels at playful motion and stylized textures.
items: Call out the art style early.
Great for fandom, gaming, and pop visuals.

specSections.title: Consistency tools
specSections.intro: Seed and negative prompts help keep a look stable across variants. For transformations, describe the start state, end state and transition clearly in the prompt.
items: Use negative prompts to remove artifacts.
Define the start state, end state and transition in text.
Export silent and score in post.

custom.specValueProp: Pika 2.2 is the stylized, silent short-clip engine: fast to iterate with seeds and negative prompts, perfect for social feeds and motion inserts.
custom.imageTitle: Related image-start workflow with Pika 2.2 (+ Nano Banana)
custom.imageIntro: This page is Text-to-Video first. Use a still only in the related Pika image workflow when composition must stay anchored.
imageFlow: Generate reference stills in Nano Banana (pose, keyframe, product illustration).
Use the related Pika image workflow with one still when available in the app.
Describe camera motion, secondary movement and effects instead of restyling.
Use seeds and negative prompts to keep composition stable across iterations.
imageWhy: Keep brand palettes/layouts while exploring motion variants
Turn poster art into animated teasers
Build looping backgrounds for streams or UI sections
custom.multishotTitle: Loops & Single-Scene Clips – Pika’s Sweet Spot
custom.multishotIntro1: Pika 2.2 excels at loops and single-scene beats.
custom.multishotIntro2: Design clean loops and simple camera paths for best results.
multishotTips: Make the last frame flow into the first (orbit, bounce, scrolling parallax)
Keep camera paths simple (pan/orbit/dolly) vs chaotic moves
One subject, one environment, one main effect per clip
Use 5s for punchy moments; 10s for a bit of build-up/payoff
Chain loops in your timeline for longer animations or carousels
strengths: Stylized-first: call out the art style early (anime / toon / comic / pixel / stylized 3D) + 2–3 palette anchors.
Loop-friendly motion: choose one repeating action (blink, sway, steam, parallax) and keep the camera mostly simple.
Consistency runs: lock a seed when iterating, and keep the phrasing stable across takes.
Cleaner outputs: use a short negative prompt to block text/logos and reduce artifacts.
For transitions, describe the start pose, landing pose and transition verb in the prompt (simple transforms > complex scenes).
boundaries: Silent output in this tier — add sound in post when needed.
Best for stylized motion and inserts; use other engines for strict photoreal output or generated sound.
Small on-screen text is unreliable — overlay critical copy in post.
safetyRules: No sexual content, and nothing involving minors.
No hateful, harassing, or graphic-violence content.
Don’t recreate real people/public figures without clear consent/rights (especially from photos/portraits).
Don’t include private personal data (faces of non-consenting people, addresses, phone numbers, documents).
Some prompts or reference images may be blocked by provider safety filters.
custom.comparisonTitle: Pika 2.2 vs MiniMax Hailuo 02 – Quick Overview
comparisonPoints: Pika 2.2: stylized, silent short clips for animation looks, loops and playful visuals in 720p/1080p.
MiniMax Hailuo 02: budget silent drafts with strong physics in 512p/768p for 6–10s beats.
Sora 2 / Wan 2.5 / Veo 3.1: switch to these when you need generated sound or more realism.

faqs.a: No. Clips are silent; add your own soundtrack or use an audio-capable engine.
5s for hooks/loops; 10s for a bit more narrative or parallax.
Yes, select 9:16 and keep the subject centered for safe crops.

faqs.q: How do seeds and negatives help?
faqs.a: Seeds keep iterations related; negative prompts block text/logos/extra characters/watermarks.
Use Sora/Veo for photoreal output or generated sound; Wan 2.5 for sound-ready clips; Sora 2 Pro/Veo 3.1 for high-end hero shots.

custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare pricing, latency, and output options across the rest of the MaxVideoAI catalog.
custom.finalPara1: Pika 2.2 in MaxVideoAI is your stylized, silent workhorse for fast loops and expressive animation.
custom.finalPara2: Draft hooks here, then translate winners to Sora or Veo when you need realism or generated sound.
custom.tipsIntro: Pika 2.2 shines when you lean into stylized looks, tight loops, and controlled iterations (seed + negative prompt).
troubleshootingItems: Looks “uncanny” / too real → push the style harder (texture, line work, palette) and avoid photoreal descriptors.
Extra limbs / weird anatomy → simplify the action, reduce secondary characters, strengthen the negative prompt.
Motion feels jittery → one subject, one action, minimal camera move; avoid fast handheld.
Loop doesn’t loop → explicitly say “seamless loop” + describe what repeats (and keep the start/end state identical).
Random text/logos appear → add “no text, no logos, no UI” to negative prompt; keep signage out of frame.

hero.paragraph: Use Pika 2.2 for prompt-led, silent stylized animation, variant testing and short social clips when the creative direction is anime, comic, pixel, toon or playful 3D.

decisionCards.title: Pika or Hailuo?
decisionCards.body: Use Pika for stylized social loops and seed variants. Use Hailuo 02 for lower-cost physics and motion checks.

decisionCards.title: Building a stylized prompt?
decisionCards.body: Lock subject, style, action and exclusions before testing variants with seeds.

decisionCards.title: Need a broader draft route?
decisionCards.body: Compare with LTX 2.3 Fast when you need a more general draft engine before production routes.

section.title: How to Write a Great Pika Prompt
section.intro: Pika performs best with stylized prompts, clear motion, and one camera move.
section.tip: Tip: duration + aspect ratio are set in the UI - your prompt controls subject, action, camera, lighting, and style. Use Negative prompt to block text/logos.

tabs.label: Quick
tabs.title: Quick prompt (fast iteration)
tabs.description: Use 1–2 sentences when you want variations.
tabs.copy:
Prompt: [Style] + [Subject + action] + [Setting] + [Camera move] + [Lighting].
Negative: [text, logos, extra limbs, blur]

tabs.label: Structured
tabs.title: Structured prompt (best for reliable results)
tabs.description: Separate information so the model can follow it consistently.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what + one action.]

Setting:
[Where + time + 2 anchors.]

Camera:
[Shot + angle + one move.]

Style:
[Art style + palette + texture.]

Negative:
[What to avoid.]

tabs.label: Pro
tabs.title: Pro prompt (ultra-specific "studio brief")
tabs.description: Use this when you need a very specific look or continuity across shots.
tabs.copy:
Brief:
[Goal + vibe.]

Subject + wardrobe:
[Key traits + materials.]

Set + lighting:
[Location + key light + palette.]

Camera:
[Framing + angle + one move + lens feel.]

Style controls:
[Animation style, texture, grain, color.]

Negative:
[Text, logos, extra characters, artifacts.]

tabNotes.body: Quick = variations. Use for fast iteration.
Structured = consistency. Use when you need reliable results.
Pro = style control. Use for precise, repeatable looks.

globalPrinciples: One subject, one action, one camera move.
Use style keywords and color palette anchors.
Add a short negative prompt for text/logos/extra limbs.
engineWhy: Short, stylized prompts keep motion clean.
Camera language helps loops feel intentional.

demo.title: Demo: a prompt for Pika 2.2
demo.prompt:
5s Pika 2.2 Text-to-Video loop in 1:1.
A tiny pixel-art wizard stirs a glowing cauldron inside a cozy cottage.
Locked medium shot, bubbles rising in a repeatable loop, books floating gently.
Warm 16-color palette, candlelight, chunky retro pixels, simple repeatable motion.
Negative prompt: text, logos, blur, extra characters, distorted hands.
notes: Clear subject in a single environment.
Simple, repeatable motion for a clean loop.
Avoid conflicting camera instructions or multiple locations.

summary.subject: Tiny pixel-art wizard
summary.action: Stirs a glowing cauldron in a seamless loop
summary.style: Retro pixel art, warm palette, candlelight
summary.output: Silent clip, no text or logos
